content.text: Codex's second pass found **6 new threads**. Let me fetch them:

content.content:
===== PRRT_kwDORp4enM6G1sVC  crates/ars-components/src/layout/carousel/mod.rs:569  reply=3350661118 =====
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Sync controlled index changes from props**

When the carousel is used in controlled mode and the parent later calls `Service::set_props` with a new `Props::index`, the machine never overrides `on_props_changed`, so the cloned `Bindable` stored here is never updated via `sync_controlled`. In that controlled-signal sync path the rendered active slide, progress text, and indicator selection remain stuck on the initial controlled value until some internal event changes state.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

===== PRRT_kwDORp4enM6G1sVI  crates/ars-components/src/layout/carousel/mod.rs:577  reply=3350661125 =====
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Clamp zero slides_per_move to one**

If a caller passes `slides_per_move: Some(0)`, every navigation path computes a zero step, so `GoToNext`, `GoToPrev`, and auto-play ticks transition without changing `current_index`; with `stop_on_interaction` a manual click can even permanently stop rotation while the slide does not move. Because the public prop is a plain `usize`, normalize this to at least one (or make it non-zero) before storing it in context.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

===== PRRT_kwDORp4enM6G1sVM  crates/ars-components/src/layout/carousel/mod.rs:840  reply=3350661131 =====
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Restart autoplay after cancelled drags**

When an autoplaying carousel receives `PointerDown`, the timer is cancelled for the drag, but if the browser then sends `PointerCancel` (for example because touch scrolling or pointer capture is interrupted), this branch only clears drag state and never re-emits `AutoPlayTimer`. The service remains in `AutoPlaying` with `auto_play_stopped == false`, but the adapter has already torn down the interval, so rotation silently stops until some unrelated resume event occurs.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

===== PRRT_kwDORp4enM6G1sVP  crates/ars-components/src/layout/carousel/mod.rs:1191  reply=3350661137 =====
**<sub><sub>![P3 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P3-lightgrey?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Treat missing autoplay config as not playing**

For a carousel with `auto_play: None`, this calculation still reports the autoplay control as playing because both flags default to false, so `auto_play_trigger_attrs()` renders the pause label and `aria-pressed="true"` even though autoplay is disabled and no timer exists. If an adapter or consumer renders the exposed `AutoPlayTrigger` part unconditionally, assistive tech announces a pause control for a feature that is not running.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

===== PRRT_kwDORp4enM6G1sVQ  crates/ars-components/src/layout/carousel/mod.rs:576  reply=3350661139 =====
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Normalize invalid slides_per_view values**

Because `slides_per_view` is a public `Option<f64>`, callers can pass `Some(0.0)` or another non-positive/non-finite value; storing it directly makes `track_offset_percent()` divide by that value, producing infinite or NaN offsets that adapters will turn into invalid transforms. Clamp or reject values to a finite positive minimum before they enter the context, as is done implicitly for the default case.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

===== PRRT_kwDORp4enM6G1sVZ  crates/ars-components/src/layout/carousel/mod.rs:693  reply=3350661150 =====
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Ignore autoplay ticks at the final non-looping page**

In a non-looping autoplay carousel already at `last_index()`, `clamp_index(current + step)` returns the same index but this branch still enters `Transitioning`. Since the adapter may not get a CSS `transitionend` when the transform does not change, the machine can become stuck in `Transitioning` and reject manual navigation; guard with `can_go_next()` or keep the machine in a resting state when the tick would not advance.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

toolUseResult.stdout:
===== PRRT_kwDORp4enM6G1sVC  crates/ars-components/src/layout/carousel/mod.rs:569  reply=3350661118 =====
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Sync controlled index changes from props**

When the carousel is used in controlled mode and the parent later calls `Service::set_props` with a new `Props::index`, the machine never overrides `on_props_changed`, so the cloned `Bindable` stored here is never updated via `sync_controlled`. In that controlled-signal sync path the rendered active slide, progress text, and indicator selection remain stuck on the initial controlled value until some internal event changes state.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

===== PRRT_kwDORp4enM6G1sVI  crates/ars-components/src/layout/carousel/mod.rs:577  reply=3350661125 =====
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Clamp zero slides_per_move to one**

If a caller passes `slides_per_move: Some(0)`, every navigation path computes a zero step, so `GoToNext`, `GoToPrev`, and auto-play ticks transition without changing `current_index`; with `stop_on_interaction` a manual click can even permanently stop rotation while the slide does not move. Because the public prop is a plain `usize`, normalize this to at least one (or make it non-zero) before storing it in context.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

===== PRRT_kwDORp4enM6G1sVM  crates/ars-components/src/layout/carousel/mod.rs:840  reply=3350661131 =====
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Restart autoplay after cancelled drags**

When an autoplaying carousel receives `PointerDown`, the timer is cancelled for the drag, but if the browser then sends `PointerCancel` (for example because touch scrolling or pointer capture is interrupted), this branch only clears drag state and never re-emits `AutoPlayTimer`. The service remains in `AutoPlaying` with `auto_play_stopped == false`, but the adapter has already torn down the interval, so rotation silently stops until some unrelated resume event occurs.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

===== PRRT_kwDORp4enM6G1sVP  crates/ars-components/src/layout/carousel/mod.rs:1191  reply=3350661137 =====
**<sub><sub>![P3 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P3-lightgrey?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Treat missing autoplay config as not playing**

For a carousel with `auto_play: None`, this calculation still reports the autoplay control as playing because both flags default to false, so `auto_play_trigger_attrs()` renders the pause label and `aria-pressed="true"` even though autoplay is disabled and no timer exists. If an adapter or consumer renders the exposed `AutoPlayTrigger` part unconditionally, assistive tech announces a pause control for a feature that is not running.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

===== PRRT_kwDORp4enM6G1sVQ  crates/ars-components/src/layout/carousel/mod.rs:576  reply=3350661139 =====
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Normalize invalid slides_per_view values**

Because `slides_per_view` is a public `Option<f64>`, callers can pass `Some(0.0)` or another non-positive/non-finite value; storing it directly makes `track_offset_percent()` divide by that value, producing infinite or NaN offsets that adapters will turn into invalid transforms. Clamp or reject values to a finite positive minimum before they enter the context, as is done implicitly for the default case.

In a non-looping autoplay carousel already at `last_index()`, `clamp_index(current + step)` returns the same index but this branch still enters `Transitioning`. Since the adapter may not get a CSS `transitionend` when the transform does not change, the machine can become stuck in `Transitioning` and reject manual navigation; guard with `can_go_next()` or keep the machine in a resting state when the tick would not advance.

input.command:
cd /Users/ericson/Workspace/Rust/ars-ui.worktrees/impl-278
git add -A
git commit -q -F - <<'EOF'
Harden carousel autoplay edges + input normalization (Codex review #716, pass 2)

Addresses 6 further Codex findings:

- Controlled mode: add on_props_changed + Event::SyncControlledIndex so a
  controlled `index` pushed via set_props updates the stored Bindable
  (sync_controlled) — active slide/progress/indicators no longer stick on the
  initial value.
- Normalize slides_per_move == 0 to 1 (a zero step made navigation a no-op
  while still able to stop rotation).
- Normalize non-finite / non-positive slides_per_view to 1.0 (prevented
  track_offset_percent div-by-zero / NaN transforms).
- PointerCancel now re-arms Effect::AutoPlayTimer when it interrupts an active
  auto-play drag (the timer cancelled on PointerDown would otherwise never
  restart, silently stopping rotation while state still read AutoPlaying).
- auto_play_trigger/indicator report "not playing" when auto_play is None
  (was rendering a pause control + aria-pressed=true for a disabled feature).
- AutoPlayTick is ignored at the final non-looping page (entering Transitioning
  with no transform change risks a missing transitionend that stalls the machine).

Tests: +8 unit tests. Spec §1.2/§1.5/§1.6 synced. cargo xci-fast green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
git rev-parse --short HEAD
git push 2>&1 | tail -2

  codex_eyes=$(echo "$reactions" | grep -E "codex.*:eyes$" || true)
  codex_thumb=$(echo "$reactions" | grep -E "codex.*:\+1$" || true)
  cur="reactions=[$reactions] threads=$threads"
  [ "$cur" != "$prev" ] && echo "[change] $cur"
  prev=$cur
  if [ -n "$codex_thumb" ]; then echo "VERDICT=approval"; exit 0; fi
  if [ -z "$codex_eyes" ] && [ "$threads" -gt 0 ]; then echo "VERDICT=needs_action threads=$threads"; exit 0; fi

done

attachment.content:
=== RUST SKILLS DISPLAY FORMAT ===
When showing Rust Skills loaded, display in this EXACT order:
1. FIRST: "🦀 Rust Skills Loaded" text
2. THEN: The Ferris crab ASCII art BELOW the text
The text must be ABOVE the crab, not below.
===

=== MANDATORY: META-COGNITION ROUTING ===

CRITICAL: You MUST follow the COMPLETE meta-cognition framework.
Partial compliance (only loading L1 skill) is NOT acceptable.

## STEP 1: IDENTIFY ENTRY LAYER + DOMAIN

### Layer 1 Signals (Start here, trace UP):
- Error codes: E0382, E0597, E0277, E0499, etc.
- Keywords: cannot be sent, moved value, borrowed, lifetime

### Layer 3 Domain Signals (MUST also load domain skill):

| Keywords in Question | Domain Skill to Load |
|---------------------|---------------------|
| Web API, HTTP, REST, axum, actix, handler, router | domain-web |
| payment, trading, fintech, decimal, currency | domain-fintech |
| CLI, command line, clap, terminal | domain-cli |
| embedded, no_std, MCU, firmware | domain-embedded |
| kubernetes, docker, grpc, microservice | domain-cloud-native |
| MQTT, sensor, IoT, telemetry | domain-iot |
| tensor, model, inference, ML | domain-ml |

**CRITICAL**: If domain keywords are present, you MUST load BOTH:
1. The Layer 1 skill (e.g., m07-concurrency)
2. The Layer 3 domain skill (e.g., domain-web)

## STEP 2: EXECUTE TRACING (MANDATORY)

For Layer 1 entry with domain context:

```
L1 Error (e.g., Rc not Send)
    ^ TRACE UP: Why this design constraint?
    ^ Load domain skill (e.g., domain-web)
    ^ Find domain constraint (e.g., "handlers run on any thread")
    v TRACE DOWN: What pattern satisfies constraint?
    v Return to L1 with context-aware solution
```

## STEP 3: MANDATORY OUTPUT FORMAT

Your response MUST include ALL of these sections:

### Reasoning Chain
```
+-- Layer 1: [specific error]
|   Problem: [error description]
|       ^
+-- Layer 3: [domain name] (domain-xxx)
|   Constraint: [what is the domain constraint]
|   Rule: [why this constraint exists]
|       v
+-- Layer 2: Design Choice
    Decision: [design decision based on domain constraint]
```

### Domain Constraints Analysis
- MUST reference specific rules from domain-xxx skill
- Explain WHY this domain has this constraint

### Recommended Solution
- Provide solution that follows domain best practices
- Not just fixing the compile error

## EXAMPLE: Web API + Send Error

Question: "Web API config sharing error: Rc cannot be sent"

CORRECT Response:
```
### Reasoning Chain
+-- Layer 1: Send/Sync Error
|   Problem: Rc<T> cannot be sent between threads
|       ^

content.content:
===== PRRT_kwDORp4enM6G2HqE  L590  reply=3350813014 =====
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Clamp controlled index during initialization**

When a caller supplies `Props.index`, this path clones the controlled `Bindable` directly and skips the `initial_index` clamp used for uncontrolled mode. With an initial controlled value past `last_index()` (for example `index=100` with 3 slides, or an index beyond the last page when `slides_per_view > 1`), the service starts with `current_index()` out of range before any prop-change sync can run, so no real item is current and `progress_text()` can render values like `Slide 101 of 3`.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

===== PRRT_kwDORp4enM6G2HqG  L650  reply=3350813016 =====
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Handle controlled-to-uncontrolled index changes**

If `Props.index` changes from `Some(...)` to `None`, `old_index != new_index` is true but the `let Some(index) = new_index` guard prevents any event from being dispatched. The context `Bindable` therefore remains controlled by the old value; subsequent `GoToNext`/`GoToSlide` calls only update the pending internal value while `current_index()`, indicators, and progress text keep reading the stale controlled index, so the carousel cannot actually become uncontrolled via `set_props`.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

===== PRRT_kwDORp4enM6G2HqH  L653  reply=3350813018 =====
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Synchronize autoplay prop changes**

When a mounted carousel receives new props that only change `auto_play` (for example `Some(...)` to `None`), this falls through with no sync event, so `ctx.auto_play` remains at the old value and any active `AutoPlayTimer` is not cancelled. In that scenario the parent has disabled autoplay, but the old interval can keep dispatching ticks and advancing slides because the machine still believes autoplay is configured.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

===== PRRT_kwDORp4enM6G2HqL  L738  reply=3350813023 =====
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Skip transitions when wrapped autoplay stays put**

For looped carousels, `can_go_next()` is always true, but `clamp_index(current + slides_per_move)` can still equal the current index, such as with a single-slide carousel or when `slides_per_move` is a multiple of `slide_count`. This enters `Transitioning` without changing the transform, the same missing-`transitionend` case the nearby comment avoids for non-looping boundaries, leaving autoplay stuck in `Transitioning` with future ticks ignored.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

===== PRRT_kwDORp4enM6G2HqP  L674  reply=3350813027 =====
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Ignore direct jumps to the current slide**

When the requested slide clamps to the current index, such as clicking the already-selected indicator or pressing `Home` while on the first slide, this still calls `navigate_to` and enters `Transitioning` even though the track transform does not change. In that no-op case adapters have no real CSS transition to report, so `TransitionEnd` may never arrive and the carousel can remain stuck in `Transitioning`, blocking all subsequent navigation.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

===== PRRT_kwDORp4enM6G2HqS  L712  reply=3350813031 =====
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Clear the paused flag when starting autoplay**

If `Event::AutoPlayStart` is sent after `Event::AutoPlayPause`, this arms `AutoPlayTimer` and moves to `AutoPlaying` but leaves `ctx.auto_play_paused` set. The timer can then advance slides while `auto_play_trigger_attrs()` still reports `aria-pressed="false"` and `item_group_attrs()` uses the paused live-region mode, so adapters using the documented start event expose a paused UI for an actively rotating carousel.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

content.text: Now P4/P5 — make `navigate_to` skip no-op transitions (return `Option`):

content.content:
<tool_use_error>String to replace not found in file.
String: /// Build the transition for a manual navigation to `idx`: enter
/// [`State::Transitioning`] and set the index, and — when
/// `stop_on_interaction` is configured — permanently stop auto-play and
/// cancel its timer. The cancellation is essential: without it the adapter's
/// recurring interval keeps running after rotation has "stopped", leaking the
/// timer and dispatching ignored [`Event::AutoPlayTick`]s.
fn navigate_to(ctx: &Context, idx: usize) -> TransitionPlan<Machine> {
    let stop = ctx.auto_play.as_ref().is_some_and(|o| o.stop_on_interaction);
    let mut plan = TransitionPlan::to(State::Transitioning).apply(move |ctx: &mut Context| {
        ctx.index.set(idx);
        if stop {
            ctx.auto_play_stopped = true;
        }
    });
    if stop {
        plan = plan.cancel_effect(Effect::AutoPlayTimer);
    }
    plan
}

        assert_eq!(

        );

        // In controlled mode the parent owns the slide index and pushes new

        let new_index = new.index.as_ref().map(|bindable| *bindable.get());
        if old_index != new_index && let Some(index) = new_index {
            return vec![Event::SyncControlledIndex { index }];
        }

        Vec::new()

content.text: xci-fast green. Committing and pushing pass-3 fixes:

input.command:
cd /Users/ericson/Workspace/Rust/ars-ui.worktrees/impl-278
git add -A
git commit -q -F - <<'EOF'
Carousel: live prop reconciliation + no-op transition guards (Codex review #716, pass 3)

Addresses 6 further Codex findings:

- init now clamps a controlled `index` past last_index (was only clamping the
  uncontrolled default), so a controlled carousel can't start out of range.
- Replace the index-only SyncControlledIndex event with a full SyncProps
  reconciliation (mirrors splitter): on_props_changed emits SyncProps on any
  change; the arm re-derives all mutable config, tracks the controlled-index
  signal including controlled->uncontrolled (sync_controlled(None)), re-clamps
  an uncontrolled index when the slide count/window shrinks, and reconciles the
  auto-play timer (enable/disable/restart-on-interval-change). Fixes stale
  ctx.auto_play leaving a live timer when the parent disables autoplay. Event
  drops Copy (carries owned Props).
- navigate_to returns None when the target equals the current index, and
  AutoPlayTick is ignored when the (clamped/wrapped) next index equals current —
  no-op transitions no longer enter Transitioning where a missing transitionend
  would strand the machine (direct jump to current slide; looped single-slide /
  slides_per_move multiple of slide_count).
- AutoPlayStart clears auto_play_paused so a start-after-pause doesn't leave the
  trigger reading aria-pressed=false / the live region in paused mode.

Tests: +8 unit tests (113 total). Spec §1.2/§1.5 synced. cargo xci-fast green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
git rev-parse --short HEAD
git push 2>&1 | tail -2

The arm calls `ctx.index.sync_controlled(props.index.map(...))`, so `Some -> None` returns the bindable to uncontrolled mode and subsequent navigation moves the visible index. reply 3350813018 'Fixed in 54b1a1fb: `on_props_changed` now emits `SyncProps` on any prop change; the arm re-derives `ctx.auto_play` (and all other mutable config) and reconciles the timer — `cancel_effect(AutoPlayTimer)` when auto-play is disabled, re-arm when enabled/interval-changed, transitioning to Idle/AutoPlaying accordingly.   threads=$(gh api graphql -f query='query($o:String!,$r:String!,$n:Int!){
    repository(owner:$o,name:$r){pullRequest(number:$n){
      reviewThreads(first:100){nodes{id isResolved comments(first:1){nodes{author{login}}}}}}}}' \
    -F o=fogodev -F r=ars-ui -F n=716 \
    --jq '.data.repository.pullRequest.reviewThreads.nodes
          | map(select(.isResolved==false and (.comments.nodes[0].author.login | test("codex";"i")))) | length' 2>/dev/null)
  codex_eyes=$(echo "$reactions" | grep -E "codex.*:eyes$" || true)
  codex_thumb=$(echo "$reactions" | grep -E "codex.*:\+1$" || true)
  cur="reactions=[$reactions] threads=$threads"
  [ "$cur" != "$prev" ] && echo "[change] $cur"
  prev=$cur
  if [ -n "$codex_thumb" ]; then echo "VERDICT=approval"; exit 0; fi
  if [ -z "$codex_eyes" ] && [ "$threads" -gt 0 ]; then echo "VERDICT=needs_action threads=$threads"; exit 0; fi
  sleep 120
done

=== RUST SKILLS DISPLAY FORMAT ===
When showing Rust Skills loaded, display in this EXACT order:
1. FIRST: "🦀 Rust Skills Loaded" text
2. THEN: The Ferris crab ASCII art BELOW the text
The text must be ABOVE the crab, not below.
===

=== MANDATORY: META-COGNITION ROUTING ===

CRITICAL: You MUST follow the COMPLETE meta-cognition framework.
Partial compliance (only loading L1 skill) is NOT acceptable.

## STEP 1: IDENTIFY ENTRY LAYER + DOMAIN

### Layer 1 Signals (Start here, trace UP):
- Error codes: E0382, E0597, E0277, E0499, etc.
- Keywords: cannot be sent, moved value, borrowed, lifetime

### Layer 3 Domain Signals (MUST also load domain skill):

| Keywords in Question | Domain Skill to Load |
|---------------------|---------------------|
| Web API, HTTP, REST, axum, actix, handler, router | domain-web |
| payment, trading, fintech, decimal, currency | domain-fintech |
| CLI, command line, clap, terminal | domain-cli |
| embedded, no_std, MCU, firmware | domain-embedded |
| kubernetes, docker, grpc, microservice | domain-cloud-native |
| MQTT, sensor, IoT, telemetry | domain-iot |
| tensor, model, inference, ML | domain-ml |

**CRITICAL**: If domain keywords are present, you MUST load BOTH:
1. The Layer 1 skill (e.g., m07-concurrency)
2. The Layer 3 domain skill (e.g., domain-web)

## STEP 2: EXECUTE TRACING (MANDATORY)

For Layer 1 entry with domain context:

```
L1 Error (e.g., Rc not Send)
    ^ TRACE UP: Why this design constraint?
    ^ Load domain skill (e.g., domain-web)
    ^ Find domain constraint (e.g., "handlers run on any thread")
    v TRACE DOWN: What pattern satisfies constraint?
    v Return to L1 with context-aware solution
```

## STEP 3: MANDATORY OUTPUT FORMAT

Your response MUST include ALL of these sections:

### Reasoning Chain
```
+-- Layer 1: [specific error]
|   Problem: [error description]
|       ^
+-- Layer 3: [domain name] (domain-xxx)
|   Constraint: [what is the domain constraint]
|   Rule: [why this constraint exists]
|       v
+-- Layer 2: Design Choice
    Decision: [design decision based on domain constraint]
```

### Domain Constraints Analysis
- MUST reference specific rules from domain-xxx skill
- Explain WHY this domain has this constraint

### Recommended Solution
- Provide solution that follows domain best practices
- Not just fixing the compile error

## EXAMPLE: Web API + Send Error

Question: "Web API config sharing error: Rc cannot be sent"

CORRECT Response:
```
### Reasoning Chain
+-- Layer 1: Send/Sync Error
|   Problem: Rc<T> cannot be sent between threads
|       ^
+-- Layer 3: Web Domain (domain-web)
|   Constraint: Handlers run on any thread
|   Rule: Shared state must be thread-safe
|       v
+-- Layer 2: Design Choice
    Decision: Use Arc<T> + State extractor

### Domain Constraints Analysis
From domain-web:
- "Rc in state" is a Common Mistake
- Web handlers require Send + Sync
- Recommended: axum State<Arc<T>> pattern

### Recommended Solution
[Code following Web best practices]
```

WRONG Response (stops at L1):
```
Problem: Rc is not Send
Solution: Use Arc
```

## SKILLS TO INVOKE

Always invoke with Skill() tool:
- Skill(rust-router) - First, to get routing
- Skill(m0x-xxx) - Layer 1 skill based on error
- Skill(domain-xxx) - Layer 3 skill based on domain keywords

===================================

content.content:
===== PRRT_kwDORp4enM6G2lch  L1478  reply=3350977157 =====
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Guard autoplay toggle when autoplay is disabled**

When `auto_play` is `None` (the default), the exposed autoplay trigger still renders as a non-playing “Start” control, but clicking it enters this `else` branch and dispatches `AutoPlayPause`, which sets `auto_play_paused = true`. If the parent later enables autoplay via `set_props`, `SyncProps` computes `want_timer` from that stale paused flag and the timer is not started, so autoplay remains inert until another manual resume; this can happen whenever the trigger is rendered before autoplay is configured.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

If the parent later enables autoplay via `set_props`, `SyncProps` computes `want_timer` from that stale paused flag and the timer is not started, so autoplay remains inert until another manual resume; this can happen whenever the trigger is rendered before autoplay is configured.

cd /Users/ericson/Workspace/Rust/ars-ui.worktrees/impl-278

git commit -q -F - <<'EOF'
Carousel: guard autoplay toggle when autoplay is disabled (Codex review #716, pass 4)

When auto_play is None the trigger renders as a non-playing "Start" control, but
clicking it dispatched AutoPlayPause and set a stale auto_play_paused flag; a
later set_props enabling autoplay then computed want_timer from that stale flag
and left the carousel inert. Guard both layers:

- on_auto_play_trigger_click is a no-op when auto_play is None.
- AutoPlayPause / AutoPlayResume early-return when auto_play is None, so the
  paused/stopped flags are only ever set while autoplay is configured (this also
  retires AutoPlayResume's now-unreachable uncontrolled fallback branch).

Tests: +3 unit tests incl. the enable-after-click regression (116 total); two
prior tests that asserted the old flag-mutating behavior updated. Spec §1.5
synced. cargo xci-fast green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
git rev-parse --short HEAD
git push 2>&1 | tail -2
echo "--- reply + resolve ---"
gh api -X POST repos/fogodev/ars-ui/pulls/716/comments -F in_reply_to=3350977157 -f body='Fixed in (pushed): `on_auto_play_trigger_click` is now a no-op when `auto_play` is `None`, and both `AutoPlayPause` and `AutoPlayResume` early-return (`ctx.auto_play.as_ref()?;`) so the paused/stopped flags are only ever set while autoplay is configured. +-- Layer 1: [specific error]

- Explain WHY this domain has this constraint

- Provide solution that follows domain best practices
- Not just fixing the compile error

### Recommended Solution
[Code following Web best practices]
```

content.text: Pass 5: 6 threads (back up from 1). Let me fetch them:

content.content:
===== PRRT_kwDORp4enM6G2-fw  L840  reply=3351116716 =====
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Ignore pointer-up events when no drag is active**

When an auto-playing carousel receives `PointerUp` without a preceding `PointerDown` recorded in `drag_start_pos`, this arm still computes `resume = true` and emits a new `AutoPlayTimer` even though the existing timer was never cancelled. A stray or adapter-level pointer-up on the viewport can therefore create duplicate autoplay intervals; this should no-op unless a drag is active, like the `PointerMove`/`PointerCancel` paths already do.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

===== PRRT_kwDORp4enM6G2-f6  L855  reply=3351116729 =====
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Apply slides_per_move to swipe navigation**

For carousels configured for page-by-page movement, e.g. `slides_per_move: Some(3)`, next/prev buttons and autoplay advance by that step, but a swipe always moves only one slide because this path hard-codes `cur + 1`/`cur - 1`. Since `slides_per_move` is documented as the number of slides to advance per navigation action, touch users get a different carousel position than keyboard/button users for the same component configuration.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

===== PRRT_kwDORp4enM6G2-f-  L958  reply=3351116734 =====
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Do not scroll when focus enters an already visible slide**

With `slides_per_view > 1`, focusing a non-leading slide that is already in the visible window still sets `current_index` to that slide's index. For example, at `current_index = 0` with two visible slides, tabbing into slide 1 shifts the carousel to start at 1, moving content unexpectedly during normal focus navigation; the focus handler should leave the index alone when `ctx.is_slide_visible(*index)` is already true.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

===== PRRT_kwDORp4enM6G2-gD  L546  reply=3351116743 =====
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Emit an index-change path for controlled carousels**

When `Props.index` is controlled, `Bindable::set` only updates the hidden internal value while `current_index()` continues to read the controlled value, and this props type has no `on_index_change` callback/effect for the parent to round-trip the requested index. In controlled usage, clicking Next/Prev or an indicator therefore enters `Transitioning` but the visible index never changes, so controlled carousels are effectively non-navigable.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

===== PRRT_kwDORp4enM6G2-gJ  L770  reply=3351116753 =====
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Honor pause_on_hover before pausing rotation**

`pause_on_hover` is exposed as part of `AutoPlayOptions`, but the machine never reads it; `AutoPlayPause` pauses any configured auto-play regardless of that setting. When an adapter dispatches the pause event from pointer hover, a carousel configured with `pause_on_hover: false` will still stop rotating, so this option needs a hover-specific event/handler or a guard that preserves manual pause behavior while respecting the hover flag.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

===== PRRT_kwDORp4enM6G2-gN  L343  reply=3351116758 =====
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Disable loop controls when there is nowhere to move**

With `loop_nav: true` and a single slide, or when `slides_per_view` already shows all slides, `can_go_prev`/`can_go_next` still return true even though every navigation clamps or wraps back to the same index. The controls therefore render enabled but clicks are no-ops because `navigate_to` rejects the unchanged index; those controls should be disabled when `last_index() == 0` (or otherwise no distinct target exists), even in looping mode.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Honor pause_on_hover before pausing rotation**

`pause_on_hover` is exposed as part of `AutoPlayOptions`, but the machine never reads it; `AutoPlayPause` pauses any configured auto-play regardless of that setting. When an adapter dispatches the pause event from pointer hover, a carousel configured with `pause_on_hover: false` will still stop rotating, so this option needs a hover-specific event/handler or a guard that preserves manual pause behavior while respecting the hover flag.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

===== PRRT_kwDORp4enM6G2-gN  L343  reply=3351116758 =====
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Disable loop controls when there is nowhere to move**

With `loop_nav: true` and a single slide, or when `slides_per_view` already shows all slides, `can_go_prev`/`can_go_next` still return true even though every navigation clamps or wraps back to the same index. The controls therefore render enabled but clicks are no-ops because `navigate_to` rejects the unchanged index; those controls should be disabled when `last_index() == 0` (or otherwise no distinct target exists), even in looping mode.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

content.text: Six more, all legitimate. Four are clear fixes (Q1 PointerUp-without-drag guard, Q2 swipe should honor `slides_per_move`, Q3 focus shouldn't scroll an already-visible slide, Q6 loop controls disabled when `last_index()==0`). But **Q4 (controlled-mode navigation round-trip)** and **Q5 (`pause_on_hover` gating)** are architectural decisions about the agnostic/adapter contract. Let me check the workspace convention for controlled value round-tripping (slider) before deciding whether Q4 has a clear answer or needs your input:

content.text: Slider's convention is clear for Q4: a `Bindable` value pairs with an `on_value_change` callback + `Effect::ValueChange` that the adapter resolves to round-trip controlled values. The carousel spec has the `Bindable` but is missing the matching callback — so Q4 is completing the established pattern. **Q5 (`pause_on_hover`)** is genuinely ambiguous in the spec (`AutoPlayPause` is documented as the "hover/focus" pause yet `FocusSlide` separately gates `pause_on_focus`), so I'll implement Q1–Q4 + Q6 and ask you about Q5.

44	/// Typed identifier for every named effect intent the carousel machine
45	/// emits.
46	///
47	/// Adapters dispatch on `effect.name` exhaustively (`match effect.name {
48	/// carousel::Effect::AutoPlayTimer => … }`) so name typos and unhandled
49	/// variants surface at compile time — the same convention used by
50	/// [`toast::single::Effect`](crate::overlay::toast::single::Effect) and the
51	/// overlay machines. The variant name itself is the contract; there is no
52	/// parallel kebab-case wire form to keep in sync.
53	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
54	pub enum Effect {
55	    /// Adapter starts (or restarts) a recurring auto-play interval of
56	    /// [`Context::auto_play`]'s `interval` that dispatches
57	    /// [`Event::AutoPlayTick`] on each fire. Emitted on mount when the
58	    /// carousel boots into [`State::AutoPlaying`] (see
59	    /// [`Machine::initial_effects`](ars_core::Machine::initial_effects)), on
60	    /// [`Event::AutoPlayStart`], on [`Event::AutoPlayResume`], and on
61	    /// [`Event::Blur`] when resuming a focus/hover pause. Cancelled on
62	    /// [`Event::AutoPlayStop`], [`Event::AutoPlayPause`], and
63	    /// [`Event::PointerDown`].
64	    AutoPlayTimer,
65	}

content.text: Clippy clean, 123 tests pass. Q1–Q4 + Q6 are done. Now Q5 is the genuine design fork — the spec is internally contradictory about where `pause_on_hover`/`pause_on_focus` gating lives. Let me get your decision before finalizing:

questions.question: Codex flagged that `AutoPlayOptions::pause_on_hover` is never read by the core — `AutoPlayPause` pauses regardless. The spec is internally inconsistent here: `AutoPlayPause` is documented as the "hover/focus" pause, yet `FocusSlide` already gates `pause_on_focus` inside the core. How should hover-pause gating work?

options.label: Core hover events (symmetric)
options.description: Add HoverStart/HoverEnd events; HoverStart pauses only when pause_on_hover is true (mirroring how FocusSlide gates pause_on_focus). AutoPlayPause/Resume become the manual trigger-button pause. Most consistent; both flags become meaningful in the core. Adds 2 public events.

options.label: Adapter gates hover
options.description: Keep AutoPlayPause/Resume generic; the adapter reads pause_on_hover and only dispatches the pause on mouseenter when true. Smaller core surface, but gating is split (core gates focus, adapter gates hover) — keeps the current inconsistency.

options.label: Adapter gates both
options.description: Remove pause_on_focus gating from FocusSlide too; the adapter gates both hover and focus, AutoPlayPause/Resume stay generic. Core exposes both flags as adapter-read config only. Fully consistent the other direction, but moves focus-pause logic out of the core.

300	### 1.5 Full Machine Implementation
301	
302	Auto-play timing is an adapter concern, not an agnostic one: the core never calls
303	`set_interval`/`set_timeout` directly. Instead it emits a typed [`Effect`] marker that adapters
304	dispatch on (`match effect.name { Effect::AutoPlayTimer => … }`) — the same convention used by
305	`toast::single::Effect`, `dialog::Effect`, `popover::Effect`, and `tooltip::Effect`. The adapter
306	resolving `Effect::AutoPlayTimer` runs a recurring interval of `ctx.auto_play.interval` that
307	dispatches `Event::AutoPlayTick`, and tears it down when the effect is cancelled.
308	
309	```rust
310	/// Typed identifier for every named effect intent the carousel machine emits.
311	///
312	/// Adapters dispatch on `effect.name` exhaustively so unhandled variants and
313	/// name typos surface at compile time.
314	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]

316	pub enum Effect {
317	    /// Adapter starts (or restarts) a recurring auto-play interval of
318	    /// `Context::auto_play.interval` that dispatches `Event::AutoPlayTick`.
319	    /// Emitted on mount when the carousel boots into `State::AutoPlaying`
320	    /// (see `initial_effects`), on `AutoPlayStart`, on `AutoPlayResume`, and
321	    /// on `Blur` when resuming a focus/hover pause. Cancelled on
322	    /// `AutoPlayStop`, `AutoPlayPause`, and `PointerDown`.
323	    AutoPlayTimer,
324	}

328	/// Build the transition for a manual navigation to `idx`: enter
329	/// `Transitioning` and set the index, and — when `stop_on_interaction` is
330	/// configured — permanently stop auto-play and cancel its timer. The
331	/// cancellation is essential: without it the adapter's recurring interval
332	/// keeps running after rotation has "stopped", leaking the timer and
333	/// dispatching ignored `AutoPlayTick`s.
334	///
335	/// Returns `None` when `idx` equals the current index: the transform would not
336	/// change, so the adapter has no CSS transition to report and `TransitionEnd`
337	/// may never arrive — entering `Transitioning` would strand the machine.
338	fn navigate_to(ctx: &Context, idx: usize) -> Option<TransitionPlan<Machine>> {
339	    if idx == ctx.current_index() { return None; }
340	    let stop = ctx.auto_play.as_ref().is_some_and(|o| o.stop_on_interaction);

lines: - `aria-live` on `ItemGroup` is `"off"` during auto-play to prevent disruptive announcements, and `"polite"` when paused or stopped.
-- Auto-play MUST pause on hover (`mouseenter`) and on focus within the carousel (`focusin`). Resumes on `mouseleave` / `focusout` unless `stop_on_interaction` triggered.

+- Auto-play pauses on hover (`mouseenter` → `HoverStart`) when `AutoPlayOptions::pause_on_hover` is set, and on focus within the carousel (`focusin` → `FocusSlide`) when `pause_on_focus` is set. Each gate is enforced in the core, not the adapter. Rotation resumes on `mouseleave` / `focusout` (`HoverEnd` / `Blur`) unless permanently stopped. The auto-play trigger button's manual pause (`AutoPlayPause`) is unconditional and independent of these options.

- Slides **outside the visible window** (`current_index` through `current_index + ceil(slides_per_view)`, wrapping when `loop_nav` is set) receive both `aria-hidden="true"` and `inert`, ensuring off-screen slides are invisible to assistive technology. With `slides_per_view > 1` every on-screen slide stays accessible — only the leading slide carries `data-ars-active`.

Carousel: swipe paging, controlled round-trip, hover-pause, no-drag/no-target guards (Codex review #716, pass 5)

Addresses 6 further Codex findings:

- PointerUp no-ops without an active drag (was re-arming a timer that was never
  cancelled → duplicate intervals on a stray pointer-up).
- Swipe navigation advances by slides_per_move, matching button/keyboard nav
  (was hard-coded ±1).
- FocusSlide only scrolls when the focused slide is not already visible
  (slides_per_view > 1 no longer shifts the track when tabbing into an on-screen
  slide).
- can_go_prev/can_go_next return false when last_index() == 0 (single slide, or
  slides_per_view shows them all), even when looping — controls disable instead
  of rendering enabled no-op clicks.
- Controlled-mode round-trip: new on_index_change callback + Effect::IndexChange,
  fired whenever the machine changes the index, so a controlled parent can push
  the requested index back through Props::index (controlled carousels were
  non-navigable). Follows the slider on_value_change/Effect::ValueChange pattern.
- pause_on_hover is now honored in the core via new HoverStart/HoverEnd events
  (HoverStart pauses only when pause_on_hover is set), mirroring how FocusSlide
  gates pause_on_focus; AutoPlayPause is reframed as the unconditional manual
  (trigger-button) pause. Adds on_root_pointer_enter/leave Api handlers.

Tests: +13 unit tests (126 total) incl. controlled round-trip via run-effect.
Spec §1.2/§1.3/§1.4/§1.5/§1.6/§3.3 synced. cargo xci-fast green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
